reviews.review_description:
When I first reviewed this game, my biggest complaint was the lack of multiplayer. The developers listened, and now multiplayer is here and works great. I even got co-location working and played with my girlfriend on two headsets, which was an amazing experience.
The physics are still the best I've seen in a VR air hockey game-far better than Air Hockey VR or Ultimate Air Hockey. The puck movement, collisions, and overall feel are incredibly realistic and smooth.
If you're looking for the closest thing to real air hockey in VR, this is it. Great job by the developers for continuing to improve the game!

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the great review.  There is definitely more to come soon!

reviews.review_description:
In 2017, I saw air hockey table at thift store and i ask my parent anc they're said yes then I play air hockey table at home but few months later, we're moving without air hockey table, I'm disappointed.

today 2026 i saw app meta quest then I'm gasp and I installed it, I'm glad, I'm gonna play air hockey table again
Dynamic. Very enjoyable. Thank-you

The actual air hockey physics feel good, but there's no replay value, and the ai player is not very competent. You are in one bland room. You can only play the same ai every time. You can not pick up the paddles. If the puck stops slightly past the middle line on the opponents side, the ai will not try to hit it and you are blocked from moving your paddle past the middle line. If you just leave your paddle directly in front of your goal, there's not enough room to score so you can never lose.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the feedback, glad you enjoyed the physics. The AI and single-player mode are intentionally simple for now as the main focus has been on real-time multiplayer and keeping both players perfectly in sync. The simple room helps maintain smooth performance on all Quest devices. but I hope to be improving it soon.

You make a good point about blocking shots by parking the paddle, and I’ll be looking to improve that. The game is still being developed and I hope to have an update out soon with some improvements and new features.

reviews.review_description: I appreciate this free demo, will definitely buy the full product.  How do you bring up the menu to change difficulty?  One thing I'd recommend is a nice sound of the air coming out of the hockey table.  That would really add to the immersion.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the great review.

You should be able bring up the menu using the menu button (3 horizontal lines on the left hand controller).  If that isn't working, it's possible the Meta / Oculus and Menu buttons have been switched in the System (From Meta Horizon Home select: Settings -> Accessibility -> Mobility -> Switch Meta and Menu buttons). If they have been switched, it doesn't currently work. That will be fixed in the next update.

I should be able to add the air sound at some point soon.

reviews.review_description: It's really good, in my opinion.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the 5-star review.  I am back to  working on the game and hope to have a few updates out in the coming months.

reviews.review_description: I love air hockey and the fact somebody made a VR game for this is amazing this game is so much fun

developer_response.body: Thank you, I'm glad you like the game so far,  I plan to have some updates out soon bringing multi-player and more.

reviews.review_title: Super and realistic   must try game
reviews.review_description: Best game  for free

developer_response.body: Thanks for the 5 star review.  I'm very glad you like it.

reviews.review_description: I love this game, I didn't play since I was 16 (long time ago 😅). This app is awsome, the physic feels real. I can't wait for onligne mode with more players and different arcade environnements.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your great review.  I've been helping a company port an exciting game to Quest2/3 for the last 2 and a half years but hoping to get back to Air Hockey Arcade very soon.

reviews.review_description:
Guys… you are really on to something here.  I just feel like you released demo a wee too soon.  Here are my opinions/feedback:

-Air Hockey table fan noise… that can’t be too difficult to capture.  Control of volume could go under “sound effects” in settings
-I went from “all in” to “I’ll finish this match but then I have to go say stuff” as soon as I realized that I could not pick up the striker.  Duuuuude.  I’ve won actual money with techniques that directly play off the ability to pick up the striker.
     -The above brings the gameplay down to somewhere between 2-3d with an amazing 3d environment.
- For us older and/or disabled folks, it would be awesome and easy to put a player height adjustment slider in settings.  I’m seated at a standard level, guardian properly calibrated, and I end up with half my face above the table.  VR glitches.  It’s relatively important to have a way to compensate on the game’s end.


In short, however, I will not be searching for any other air hockey game.  It’s obvious that y’all know what you’re doing and I’m confident that I’d be happy to pay for the full version once it’s released.

developer_response.body:
Hey, thanks for the review and I'm sorry for taking so long to respond.  Paying work keeps getting in the way of working on this game.

Anyway, in response to your comments.

- Air Hockey table fan noise.  Yes you are right, I did have it in an earlier version of the game but took it out as it was annoying.  I will try to get an update out soon.

-  Not being able to pick up the mallet / striker whatever you want to call it.  Yes I would love to implement this, but it's tricky.  The game engine is completely written from scratch and the physics system needs quite a major update in order to be able to lift the Mallet.  But it is a major one on my wish list too.

- There is a slider to adjust the table height, on the Options menu screen, if that's not enough please let me know in the discord: https://discord.gg/rpC95Dgtdd

Thanks again for your review.

Claire (Dev).

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: I found the setting for table height. 

This game nails the physics of air hockey perfectly, and that's the most important thing in any game, for it to feel real. This feels about as real as you can get. The graphics are also really sharp, the floor looks amazingly real, every crack, etc. Now the overall game just needs a few more bells and whistles. My first request would be a way to change the table height. I'm tall and I felt like I had to bend over a little too much. After that I'm looking forward to all the things that have already been mentioned: different environments, multiplayer with chat, music options, different colors and sizes of pucks and paddles. Someone mentioned arcade games, it would be amazing to have an arcade game in one corner or other games in the room, even something simple like darts. Those photos on the walls are screaming to be user customized. Oh, one last thing. A couple of times the AI opponent just zoned out and stood there and wouldn't hit the puck anymore. He would slide his paddle toward the puck a little but never hit it. I then had to quit the game and go back in. Looking forward to the full game!

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the great review.  I'm glad you found the slider to adjust the table height :)

I'm working on Multiplayer right now, specifically getting the chat stuff working, so hopefully it'll be ready soon.

Yes, the pictures on the wall will be user customized eventually.  That was always the plan, and I would love to put an arcade game in the corner too.  It's just a question of finding the time to get it all done :)

Yes, there are few small bugs with the AI, you shouldn't need to quite the game if that happens, there is an "End Game" button that pops up on the menu you can just press that if there is a problem.  The AI "usually" works things out though.  And when I have done all the other million and one things, I plan to improve the AI too.

reviews.review_description:
I loved playing table hockey in the arcades in the '80s & '90s.  This game brought me back to my favorite arcade slamming that plastic puck back & forth.  

The sounds & the physics are so close to reality, that you think you're playing the real thing.  

I'd love to see this in a multiplayer game.  I'd love to play friends, family & others in this game.  I'd also like to see different rooms to play in.  It would be cool to play in rooms that look like an arcade filled with coin-op games, with the sounds of an arcade going off around you.  Maybe also have the table centered on a full-sized hockey rink. 

I highly recommend getting this game!

developer_response.body: Thanks for the great review.  I'm hoping to have an update out in the next day or so that will provide experimental multiplayer functionality.  It's unlikely to be perfect initially but I want to get it out for people to try, so I can find where the issues are.

reviews.review_description: I really love this game, I would pay for it for sure. For technical point of view I would add feature which allows you to raise the paddle. All in all the game is responsive and relaxing. It would be great to have a new environments and for example a Youtube widget with screen, so that it would be possible to set a playlist while you play the game.

developer_response.body:
Hello, thank you for the great review.  I have so many things that I want to add to this game, but have become totally sidetracked by the multi-player support which is proving much trickier than I anticipated.

I'm hoping it wont be too much longer (otherwise I'll run out of money ;) ) and then I can get back to working on more fun content again.

reviews.review_description: Probably one of the Best app lab games on quest, Nice physics, Nice graphics! 👍👍

Fun game, but where’s do I find the settings?   Nothing I push brings up a menu.  

Also, what’s the ETA for playing others with the app?

developer_response.body:
Thank you for the review.  To bring up the menu you have to press the menu button on  the left controller, the one with the 3 horizontal lines on it.  If it's not working for you, there may be an issue I've missed.

Please contact support@guiltydog.co.uk or alternatively if you go to my discord server and I can try to find out what is going wrong: https://discord.com/invite/rpC95Dgtdd

As for ETA for playing with others, I hope to have something out in 'experimental' form in the next week or so.

reviews.review_description:
Hi really enjoyed that it was like going back to being a teenager in the bowling alley arcade.
 I think areas of improvement are maybe a harder difficulty level as easy medium and hard we’re not that difficult to beat and maybe some classic eighties music playing in the background then it would feel really retro good game look forward to playing it again in the future.

developer_response.body: Thankyou for the great review.  I'm just finishing up an update that will bring experimental multiplayer support.  I play on adding music soon, and I do want to improve the AI as well.

reviews.review_description: This game is a must have for anyone who is even interested in hockey, I just have one recommendation. Change the radius of the Ai area… what I mean by that’s is allow the ai to move past the blue line like the player can. I’ve had to sit for 2 minutes waiting for the puck to cross the red line so I can strike it. Otherwise good job

This game has a lot of potential. I like the feel of it. It needs multiplayer, better avatars and music. I could see this as game that goes into my rotation with Walkabout Mini Golf and Eleven Table Tennis.

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the review, I'm glad you like it so far.

Multi-player is currently the main focus of development, I can't say when it will be done but I'm working on it.

I'm not an artist and I don't have the skill or budget to make better avatars.  As such, this uses Oculus's avatar system, so feel free to moan at them if you don't think they are good enough they have way more money than me ;)

A music player is planned (hence the speakers).  I'd hoped to get it working for launch but there were some technical issues.  My plan is to have it play MP3s you upload to your device, although I may try to write some tracks as well.

Please visit our SideQuest page to follow future development: https://sidequestvr.com/app/2764/air-hockey-arcade

reviews.review_description: Great game, feels like the real thing. You get distracted by how good it looks. Also offers 120 Hz support. Online multilayer would be cool. Overall great game and would recommend

developer_response.body:
Thanks for the great review.  I'm glad you like how it looks.  Yes I snuck 120Hz support in just before it finally got approved.

Online multi-player is on the way, but I wanted to get the single player game out as a demo as soon as I felt I could.

reviews.review_description:
I enjoyed playing this game (thanks to the devs for letting us try this out). The mechanics were easy to master and the game was fun. There were a couple of things that I feel may make this a more enjoyable experience, if I may make a couple of suggestions:

If you could make the table adjustable. I was at a slight discomfort with where the table was set and my arms length. 

There wasn't a lot in the way of Sound F/X. Maybe more of a sound when someone scores, background music and sounds  (maybe even access to Google for people to enjoy their playlists.)

This will be fun for people to play multiplayer, but it would be nice to keep the AI  for single players to play. Maybe change the opponent to a random character instead of player your own Avatar. 

This was really fun. Thank you for the opportunity to let me try it out.

developer_response.body:
Hi, thanks for the great review.

You are definitely not the first to suggest table adjustment :)

Multiplayer is in development as I write this and a music player in some form is planned, hence the speakers in the room.  I'm not quite sure what form it will take but I'm open to ideas.

Feel free to join our discord if you would like to follow future development and make suggestions. https://sidequestvr.com/app/2764/air-hockey-arcade
